So every company in your portfolio has agencies they hate working with or they just fired one and look for another one.
And that was always me.
Every single company, I'm like asking all my friends and all my peers, hey, have you worked with anyone on this?
And most of the time I would just go build a team myself, except I, you know, marketers have a shelf life of about 12 months.
Yeah.
Companies.
So if it takes six months for them to build a team and six months just start to sell signals, they're fired by that.
Yeah.
That's why they prefer to like have a partner that.
It's just like ready to go, you know?
And from a fundraising perspective, you've been incredibly capital efficient, and you could probably do this without having to raise more capital.
Yeah.
There are some constraints, so I'm not advocating for that.
But as you think about this raise, you're going to raise the capital, so that's out of the question.
How are you going to optimize who you take money from?
And let me clarify why I'm asking a question, because if you're looking for something that we're not, I'm going to be straightforward.
We don't make a lot of investments.
It's like each partner will make one or two new investments a year.
As a firm, we make five new investments a year, seven.
So we know that we're not the right firm for everyone, and not everyone is the right partner for us.
The ones that we find that connection, we become friends, and we work for years.
We know their kids, their spouses, their dogs.
And everything, it's not for everyone, what I'm trying to say.
So I'm going to tell you.

We kind of feel mutually on that front, as far as like, we're not for everyone either, you know, in the sense that like, we, the last thing we want is someone that's going to try to put us in a pattern match, old school pattern matching and say, you are this, you need to be SaaS, you need to be do this, right?
Like, our business doesn't look like anything.
Yes, we're service, we're not hiding that, but we're also 70% margin.
We grew up with $3 million before we wrote a single line of code.
Like, you know, it's like we, like, it's a different world and we're not coming at it from a bias.

What we're coming at it from is like, I'm replacing a service and I will do whatever's necessary as long as it's efficient and it's the right thing to do in order to go be the best service possible to these companies, right?

And when you come at that from that perspective versus trying to optimize for VC perspective, it looks different, but like, and it's messy at times, but it's also like we're building the right perimeter.
I think with the right.
You know, but also like you take like a Harvey AI and others, like they were closing 150 grand contracts, but they had to offer these firms to co-sell with them, right?
Like we're closing, we closed some contracts, we closed RAMP in five days.
It was a $270,000 contract, you know, in literally one 30-minute meeting.
We had one 30-minute meeting and they signed five days later.
Yeah.
Yeah, so there's- And is now an angel.
And so for us, like we only have one investor, which is Karam, on our board.
You know, we have a couple of angels as part of that round.
And that was the first dollars we ever raised.
And so for this round, like we are not trying to like hype up valuations or do anything like that.
Like for us, like it's way more important, like who we're getting married to than how expensive the wedding is going to be.
Kind of thing, you know, but obviously like I have a fiduciary duty to also do the best thing for the company.
We need to, you know, obviously, but, but like, but, but for us, we're capital efficient, but it, we, this is the biggest opportunity that we'll ever be in this space.
And we would just, I think we're just best position and we'd be super dumb not to just effing go for it and win gas, you know, we're competitive bunches, you know, in Outland America.
Where are you, where are you in the fundraising process?

Yeah, so we're talking to about 10 firms in all transparency, but we're not, like, I've talked to over 50 since Series A, you know, like, it was just like, spread them out because it's very intense, you know, and, and I think for us, we've been very selective.
One, we're not talking to anyone that has invested in Profound and any of the visibility players, because for us, if they believe that that's how you win, they're not the right partners for us.
Two, we're only talking to people that believe in services software, you know, and, and so it's like a, a small group we've had, probably most of them, like.

Three to five meetings, including one partnership meeting this morning, and then some of them are starting to do like technical diligence today, tomorrow, and, you know, but we're not trying to accelerate the process, and we have no problem taking another week or two, and, you know, this is a mutually important decision, and we're not asking for a tiny little check, that's just somebody's gonna, you know, so, but we have a data room, we have everything super clean and organized, and, you know, as well, so to also not waste anyone's time.
Yeah, so if you could share the data room, we can do some work this weekend, and let you know, again, we're not gonna waste your time, if we feel that we're not gonna get there, I'll let you know.
However, I would say, we believe in the category, we believe, I we've been talking about services for a long time, even before it became a thing.
And, and, we, we really like the way you're approaching.
This business.
It doesn't feel like a hype-y business.
It feels like, even in this call, it's like you're being very transparent.
And that's honestly how we like to build relationships.
There's going to be some bumps along the road.
And we want the people that we work with to feel comfortable.
It's like, oh, I messed up.
This didn't work.
Let's figure out how we address it.
Yeah, that's perfect.
That sounds good.
I'll share everything right after this call.
We have our investment memo data room and a bunch of things.
But obviously, let me drop my phone number.
And I'll send it in the email.
But text me, WhatsApp me as well.
Because I know sometimes, like, you know, if there's something I need to respond really quickly, I can kind of jump on it right away.
I'm like, you know, spending time with them or whatever.
But I'll send everything over.
And
I like that you're on WhatsApp.
Yeah, and then say hi to Kevin Spain.
haven't talked to him since upkeep days, you know, but you can, if you guys want to ask him about me, hopefully he has a few good things to remember from me, but if not.
I mean, for upkeep, I mean, I know the company, I've known it for a long time.
I think sometimes I say it's like, it's not a company, it's you.
Here, I think it's, like, I've seen.
When he, so many organ rejections, we're like, oh, it's a good organ, but it's like, oh, it's been rejected by the organization.
But sometimes it's the organ that just jumps out of the body.
So it's not that it's being pushed out.
It's like the organ that you're trying to stuff in, it's like great, great heart, but it just jumps out of the body.
Yeah, exactly.
But, well, I appreciate it.
I'm sure we'll talk super soon, and then I'll work on sending you guys something right away.
Sounds good.
Marcel, thank you very much.
Thank you.
a great weekend.
Thanks so much, Marcel.
Thanks, George.
Talk soon.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss GrowthX's AI-powered content marketing platform and potential investment opportunity with Emergence Capital.](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX has built an AI-powered platform to replace traditional marketing agencies, achieving $11M run rate in 1 year with 70%+ gross margins](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [The company uses AI workflows and expert human oversight to deliver high-quality, customized content marketing at scale](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=1335.0)
  - [Currently raising a round and selectively speaking with VCs who understand their unique service-software hybrid model](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=1362.0)
  - [Emergence expressed interest in learning more and continuing discussions](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=2401.0)

## Topics

### GrowthX Background and Traction

  - [Founded by Marcel Santilli (ex-CMO at several tech companies) and an experienced CTO](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=492.0)
  - [Reached $11M run rate in 1 year since founder went full-time](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [70+ person team, including many experienced tech/marketing veterans](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=777.0)
  - 70%+ gross margins, burned \<$3M to date
  - [Notable clients include Ramp, Augment Code, Webflow, Abnormal Security](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=1204.0)

### Product and Technology

  - [AI-powered platform for end-to-end content marketing execution](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=1335.0)
  - [Combines AI workflows (e.g. research, fact-checking) with human expert oversight](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=1445.0)
  - [Internal tools for context engineering, content creation, and quality control](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=1509.0)
  - [Not currently offered as self-serve SaaS, focused on managed service model](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=1362.0)

### Business Model and Strategy

  - [Replacing traditional marketing agencies with tech-enabled managed service](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=1939.0)
  - [8-week onboarding sprint to deeply understand client needs](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=1682.0)
  - [Long-term vision to build expert marketplace and modular apps](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=1908.0)
  - [Not competing directly with existing martech tools](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=1939.0)

### Fundraising Status

  - [Selectively speaking with \~10 VC firms](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=1939.0)
  - [Looking for investors who understand service-software hybrid model](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=2107.0)
  - [Have completed several partner meetings, some firms starting technical diligence](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=2245.0)
  - [Not rushing process, prioritizing finding the right long-term partner](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=2107.0)

### Emergence's Initial Reaction

  - [Expressed interest in the category and GrowthX's approach](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=2593.0)
  - [Appreciate the transparency and non-hype focus on building a real business](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=2346.0)
  - [Want to review materials and potentially continue discussions](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=2401.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Marcel to share data room and investment materials with Emergence team](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=2374.0)
  - [Emergence to review materials over the weekend and follow up](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=2401.0)
  - [Potential technical diligence and further discussions if mutually interested](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=2401.0)
  - [Marcel to text contact info for quick communication if needed](https://fathom.video/share/PMRvzfZjtBjEogXuCzgaZzoSqj7wr3tV?tab=summary&timestamp=2380.0)

action_items.description: Review GrowthX data room, investment memo over weekend; follow up w/ Marcel
Send investment memo, data room access, contact info (incl. phone/WhatsApp) to Santi/George via email
